title: stuckenia filiformis subsp. alpina
intro: Stuckenia filiformis subsp. alpina, also known as Alpine Waterwort, is a submersed aquatic plant species belonging to the Potamogetonaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and urinary problems. The plant has been found to possess diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including alleviating symptoms of kidney stones and reducing inflammation in the urinary tract. Alpine Waterwort can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy in traditional medicine.
